prompt: The generic method causes an unchecked cast warning that leads to ClassCastException. What is the bug?
choices: Object[] cannot hold String and Integer simultaneously
The unchecked cast (T) arr[0] always returns the object as-is at runtime due to type erasure; the ClassCastException occurs when assigning to Integer n because arr[0] is a String
firstElement() must declare the array parameter as T[]
Generic return types always cause ClassCastException
explanation: Due to type erasure, (T) arr[0] is a no-op at runtime — T is erased to Object. The actual cast happens at the call site when assigning to Integer n. arr[0] is a String, so ClassCastException is thrown. Fix: pass the expected class and cast explicitly: Class<T> clazz, then clazz.cast(arr[0]).
